heroPoems.title: This is my letter to the world
heroPoems.firstStanza:
This is my letter to the world,
That never wrote to me,—
The simple news that Nature told,
With tender majesty.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: Bodies Fall, Being Remains
heroPoems.firstStanza:
As a person changes worn-out clothes,
Being puts down one body
and takes up another.

heroPoems.title: The Return of the Children
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Neither the harps nor the crowns amused, nor the cherubs' dove-winged races--
Holding hands forlornly the Children wandered beneath the Dome,
Plucking the splendid robes of the passers-by, and with pitiful faces
Begging what Princes and Powers refused:--'Ah, please will you let us go home?'
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus I, 3
heroPoems.firstStanza:
A god can do it. But tell me how
should a man follow him through the narrow lyre?
His senses divide him.
At the crossing of two heart-paths there is no temple for Apollo.

heroPoems.title: The cup returns because thirst remembers.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The cup returns because thirst remembers.
I keep refusing, and still I drink.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: The Four Great
heroPoems.firstStanza:
There is something whole
before heaven and earth.
Silent.
Formless.
Alone.
Unchanging.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: I laugh when I hear that the fish in the water is thirsty
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I laugh when I hear that the fish in the water is thirsty:
You do not see that the Real is in your home, and you wander from
forest to forest listlessly!
Here is the truth!  Go where you will, to Benares or to Mathura;
if you do not find your soul, the world is unreal to you.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Thou hast made me known to friends whom
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Thou hast made me known to friends whom I knew not. Thou hast given me
seats in homes not my own. Thou hast brought the distant near and made
a brother of the stranger.
I am uneasy at heart when I have to leave my accustomed shelter; I
forget that there abides the old in the new, and that there also thou
abidest.

heroPoems.title: Soft Answer
heroPoems.firstStanza:
A soft answer
turns away wrath.
A harsh word
stirs anger.

heroPoems.title: Lady you hold my heart and still you do not listen
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Lady you hold my heart and still you do not listen
I spoke clearly the fault was never in the words
My drunk mind made a storm
but each wave called your name

collections.name: The Weight of Small Things
collections.tagline: For nights that move without noise.
collections.theme: A gathering of poems where memory and present tense overlap gently.

featuredPoem.title: I Know My Redeemer Lives
featuredPoem.firstLines:
My words are poured out,
my friends have become my scorn,

poems.title: I am only waiting for love to give
The moon shines in my body, but my blind eyes cannot see it

collections.name: A Window Left Open
collections.tagline: For when the kettle cools untouched.
collections.theme: Poems that move through corridors, stairwells, and rooms as emotional architecture.

poems.title: I had gone a-begging from door to door

collections.name: What Stays After Rain
collections.tagline: For when footsteps feel far away.
collections.theme: A restrained current of longing that never announces itself directly.

featuredPoem.title: Knowing the Invisible
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Those who know
rarely need to announce it.

poems.title: Singer scatter fresh notes over the night
